title: Reopen a cancelled workflow — resume from where it was cancelled
purpose: Widen the reopen operation built in AD-012 to accept a terminal-Cancelled run, not only a terminal-Failed one, so a deliberately cancelled workflow can be brought back and resumed from exactly where it was when it was cancelled. A cancel is recorded as a single WorkflowCancelled terminal and the running process is killed — it does NOT fail or record a terminal for the activities that were in flight; those are simply abandoned mid-execution. So reopening a cancel is not 're-drive a failed step' (there is no failed step) — it is 'supersede the cancellation, project Running again, and let the steps that were in flight at cancel time re-dispatch via the existing recovery path, while every already-recorded step replays from history unchanged.' This brief rides the AD-011 foundation and the AD-012 operation unchanged except for the precondition predicate and the reopened-set rule for a cancel; it is engine-only, and the AO-007 wire/CLI surface carries the widened behaviour with no edit.
task: Extend the AD-012 engine reopen operation to accept a terminal-Cancelled run. (1) Widen the reopen precondition: the operation proceeds when current_lease_terminal is Some(WorkflowFailed) OR Some(WorkflowCancelled), and still rejects not-found, not-terminal, terminal-for-Completed/TimedOut, and already-Running with the existing typed EngineError::InvalidState. (2) Define the reopened-activity-set for a cancel: a cancel records no terminal activity failures, and — unlike a failure — the operator does NOT want to re-drive steps the workflow already handled and moved past; so the reopened set written into WorkflowReopened for a cancelled run is EMPTY, and the steps that were in flight at cancel time (scheduled/started, no terminal) re-dispatch through the existing ResumeLive cursor-exhaustion path exactly as crash recovery does. (3) Resume semantics: every already-recorded outcome (including any activity failure the workflow caught and continued past before the cancel) replays from history unchanged; only the genuinely in-flight-at-cancel step is re-dispatched live; a run cancelled while parked on a signal or timer reopens back into that wait. (4) Namespace affinity, single-continuous-Recorder discipline (invariant #3), and the WorkflowReopened append are reused from AD-012 unchanged. (5) Edge cases: a reopened-from-cancel run is cancellable again and reopenable again; history accumulates Cancelled -> Reopened -> (Cancelled | Failed | Completed) with the projection always the last lifecycle event; a server restart after a cancel-reopen re-drives via the existing startup recovery path with no special-casing. Cover every behaviour with real runtime tests.

requirements.title: Widen the reopen precondition to accept terminal-Cancelled
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL accept a reopen of a run whose current-lease terminal is WorkflowCancelled in addition to WorkflowFailed, and SHALL still reject a run that is not found, not terminal, terminal for Completed or TimedOut, or already Running, with the existing typed EngineError.
acceptance: The reopen precondition predicate proceeds when aion_core::current_lease_terminal is Some(Event::WorkflowFailed { .. }) OR Some(Event::WorkflowCancelled { .. }) for the target run; the failed-reopen behaviour from AD-012 is unchanged.

Completed and TimedOut current-lease terminals are still rejected with EngineError::InvalidState (message naming the actual status); not-terminal and already-Running are still rejected; absent history is still WorkflowNotFound.

The reset-aware terminality and the WorkflowReopened supersession from AD-011 apply identically — a WorkflowReopened recorded after WorkflowCancelled projects Running under the existing last-lifecycle-event-wins scan, exactly as it does after WorkflowFailed.

Real runtime tests over InMemoryStore cover: reopen of a terminal-Cancelled run is accepted; reopen of a Completed/TimedOut run is rejected; the failed-reopen acceptance and all AD-012 rejections still hold (no regression).

requirements.title: Empty reopened-set for a cancel; in-flight steps resume via recovery
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL write an empty reopened-activity-set into the WorkflowReopened event for a cancelled run, so that no already-recorded step is re-driven and the steps in flight at cancel time re-dispatch through the existing ResumeLive cursor-exhaustion path.
acceptance: For a current-lease terminal of WorkflowCancelled the reopened set is empty: a cancel records no terminal ActivityFailed, and a step the workflow already caught and moved past (its recorded outcome is in history) is NOT re-driven — its recorded outcome replays unchanged.

A step that was scheduled/started but had no terminal at cancel time (genuinely in flight when the process was killed) is re-dispatched by the existing recovery path — the cursor reaches it, finds no recorded outcome, and returns ResumeLive — without that step appearing in the reopened set (it is handled by the same path crash recovery already uses for in-flight activities).
The WorkflowReopened marker's role for a cancel is purely to supersede the WorkflowCancelled terminal and project Running; the cursor reset rule (AD-011) sees an empty set and performs no resets.

Real runtime tests cover: a cancel with one in-flight step (re-dispatched live on reopen); a cancel after a caught-and-continued activity failure (the caught failure replays from history, is NOT re-driven); a cancel while parked on a signal/timer (reopens back into the wait).

requirements.title: Resume the cancelled run from where it was, with namespace affinity
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL re-drive a reopened-from-cancel run through the existing AD-012 respawn-and-replay path, replaying every recorded outcome unchanged and re-dispatching only the in-flight-at-cancel step, in the workflow's own namespace re-derived from history.
acceptance: After the WorkflowReopened append, the run respawns and registers as Resident exactly as AD-012's failed-reopen does, reusing the single continuous Recorder (invariant #3) and the register_recovered_resident path with no second writer.

Replay returns every recorded result for completed steps; the in-flight-at-cancel step (if any) hits ResumeLive and re-dispatches keyed by (namespace, activity_type), the namespace re-derived from history — a remote-namespace cancelled workflow reopens on a remote worker, never local.
A run cancelled while suspended on a signal or timer reopens into Running and re-arms that wait rather than re-dispatching an activity.
A real test pins the namespace-affinity guarantee for the cancel case, and a returned WorkflowHandle reflects the now-Running run.

requirements.title: Edge cases: re-cancel, re-reopen, mixed chains, restart durability
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL handle a reopened-from-cancel run safely across re-cancellation, repeated reopen, mixed terminal chains, and a server restart, with the projection always the last lifecycle event.
acceptance: A run reopened from cancel can be cancelled again, and reopened again; history accumulates WorkflowCancelled -> WorkflowReopened -> WorkflowCancelled and the projection is always the last event.

Mixed chains are sound: Cancelled -> Reopened -> Failed is reopenable (per AD-012), and Failed -> Reopened -> Cancelled is reopenable (per this brief); the exactly-one-terminal-per-lease guard from AD-011 still holds within each lease.

A second reopen of an already-Running (reopened-from-cancel) run returns EngineError::InvalidState (already Running) and appends no second marker; two racing reopens never both append (the recorder's expected-seq discipline).

After a cancel-reopen, a simulated restart (rebuild from store + run startup recovery) re-derives the run as Running and re-drives the in-flight step through the existing path, with no cancel-specific special-casing in startup.
Real runtime tests over InMemoryStore cover the re-cancel/re-reopen chain, both mixed terminal chains, the double-reopen reject, and the restart-after-cancel-reopen durability case.

boundaries: SHALL NOT add reopen for Completed or TimedOut runs — Cancelled and Failed are the only reopenable terminals after this brief; TimedOut is a named follow-on.
SHALL NOT change the WorkflowReopened event shape, the status projection, the cursor reset rule, or the reset-aware terminality — those are AD-011 and are reused unchanged.

SHALL NOT alter the AD-012 failed-reopen behaviour, its reopened-set computation for a failed run, or its single-continuous-Recorder discipline — this brief only widens the precondition and adds the empty-set cancel rule.
SHALL NOT add or change the ReopenWorkflow RPC, the client op, or the aion reopen CLI — AO-007 carries the widened engine behaviour unchanged with no edit there.
SHALL NOT introduce automatic retry, classification, or any RetryPolicy plumbing — that is the L1+L2 follow-on.
SHALL NOT re-drive any step that has a recorded outcome in the cancelled run — a caught-and-continued activity failure replays from history; only the genuinely in-flight-at-cancel step re-dispatches.
verification: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes with no #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses.
cargo test across aion-core, aion-store, aion-store-libsql, aion, and aion-server passes, including all new cancel-reopen tests.
Every touched file remains within the 500-code-line limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace); split by responsibility if reopen.rs would exceed it.

The widened precondition, the empty-set cancel rule, the in-flight-step resume, the caught-failure-replays-unchanged guarantee, the namespace-affinity guarantee, and every edge case are each proven by real runtime tests (no #[ignore], no env gate).
The AD-012 failed-reopen tests continue to pass unchanged, proving no regression to the failed path.
Rigorous Opus sub-agent review against this brief, AD-012, AD-011, and docs/WORKFLOW-REOPEN-DESIGN.md before the work is considered done.
